name: Cluster Circuit Court
description: A precedent-driven expert system for distributed-job diagnosis. The online lower court applies deterministic rules to replayable case snapshots and escalates uncovered failures to a ReAct circuit court. Offline, Coco turns resolved misses into candidate rule changes, while Aime, regression replay, and final human review form the promotion gate before validated precedents return to the deterministic path.

name: MIT 6.5840 Distributed Systems Labs
description: Implemented a MapReduce coordinator-worker runtime, a distributed key/value service, and core Raft consensus in Go, including RPC-based coordination, leader election, randomized election timeouts, and heartbeat handling.

name: Automated CI/CD Pipeline with Quality Gates
description: Independent course project that provisioned a complete GCP delivery environment with Terraform, GKE, Jenkins, SonarQube, Dataproc, and Helm. Incremental quality gates conditionally triggered Hadoop jobs and reduced deployment time from 120 minutes to 15 minutes in the course benchmark environment.

name: High-Performance Dynamic Memory Allocator
description: Implemented segregated free lists, mini-block optimization, footer elimination, and constant-time coalescing in C, achieving 74% memory utilization and 9,662 Kops/sec throughput while exceeding the course performance target by 33%.

name: Scalable Sentiment Analysis App
description: Deployed a three-tier sentiment-analysis application on GKE with independently scalable React, Spring Boot, and Python services, using Kafka for asynchronous communication between application and logic layers.
